questions.questionText: Exclusive breastfeeding is recommended for the first:
questions.optionB: 6 months (WHO/IAP — no water, formula, or complementary food; only breast milk + medicines/vitamins if needed)
questions.explanation: Exclusive breastfeeding: first 6 months (WHO/IAP/UNICEF recommendation). Only breast milk, no water, formula, or complementary food. Breast milk: contains IgA (mucosal immunity), lactoferrin, lysozyme, oligosaccharides (prebiotic). Colostrum: first 3-5 days, rich in IgA, protein, low fat. Complementary feeding: start at 6 months (continue breastfeeding until 2 years). Formula: if breast milk unavailable. Benefits: reduced infections, allergies, SIDS, maternal benefits. Reference: OP Ghai — Breastfeeding.

questions.questionText: Kwashiorkor is a form of severe acute malnutrition characterized by:
questions.optionB: Edema (pitting, bilateral pedal → generalized), flaky paint dermatosis, flag sign hair, moon facies, fatty liver, preserved subcutaneous fat
questions.explanation: Kwashiorkor: protein-deficiency predominant SAM. Features: bilateral pitting EDEMA (hallmark — starts feet, spreads), flaky paint dermatosis (hyperpigmented → desquamation), flag sign (alternating bands of light/dark hair — reflects periods of adequate/inadequate nutrition), moon facies, hepatomegaly (fatty liver), apathy/irritability. Subcutaneous fat: PRESERVED (unlike marasmus). Marasmus: calorie-deficiency SAM — severe wasting, loss of subcutaneous fat, 'old man facies,' muscle wasting, NO edema, alert/hungry. Marasmic-kwashiorkor: combined features. Reference: OP Ghai — PEM.

questions.questionText: Vitamin A deficiency in children first manifests as:
questions.optionB: Night blindness (nyctalopia — earliest clinical manifestation, followed by Bitot's spots, then xerosis, then keratomalacia)
questions.explanation: Vitamin A deficiency stages (WHO): XN: night blindness (earliest). X1A: conjunctival xerosis. X1B: Bitot's spots (foamy, white, triangular patches on bulbar conjunctiva — pathognomonic). X2: corneal xerosis. X3A: corneal ulceration (<1/3). X3B: corneal ulceration (≥1/3) / keratomalacia (liquefactive necrosis — irreversible blindness). XS: corneal scar. XF: xerophthalmic fundus. National prophylaxis (India): Vitamin A solution — 1 lakh IU at 9 months, then 2 lakh IU every 6 months until 5 years. Reference: OP Ghai — Vitamin A Deficiency.
questions.conceptTag: Vitamin A night blindness Bitot spots
